title: ViT-Lite: Compact Vision Transformers via Token Merging
abstract: We introduce ViT-Lite, a family of vision transformer variants that achieve a 3x reduction in compute by progressively merging tokens at each layer. Across ImageNet-1k, COCO detection, and ADE20K segmentation, ViT-Lite matches or outperforms standard ViT baselines while using 60% fewer FLOPs. We further analyze how the optimal merge schedule varies with task and provide pretrained checkpoints. Code and pretrained models are released.

title: DiffSeg: Diffusion Models for Open-Vocabulary Segmentation
abstract: We present DiffSeg, a framework that repurposes pretrained text-to-image diffusion models for open-vocabulary semantic segmentation without any segmentation supervision. By extracting cross-attention maps at multiple diffusion timesteps and applying a novel boundary-aware refinement that uses latent-space gradient information, DiffSeg outperforms prior zero-shot baselines on PASCAL VOC, COCO, and ADE20K. We further show that segmentation quality scales with diffusion model capacity, suggesting a path to even stronger zero-shot segmentation as base models improve.

title: Robust 3D Reconstruction from Sparse Views via Geometric Self-Supervision
abstract: Sparse-view 3D reconstruction remains challenging due to depth ambiguity and limited multi-view geometric constraints. We propose a self-supervised geometric consistency loss that enforces multi-view agreement at both feature and depth levels, enabling reconstruction from as few as 3 input views. Experiments on DTU and BlendedMVS show consistent improvements over recent NeRF-based and Gaussian-Splatting baselines under sparse-view settings, with PSNR gains of 1.2-2.4 dB.
